fields.label: Target Instance Scaling Rule

log.why: Some assignable units scale separately from target count, such as one extra dart per slot above 1.

fields.comment: Target Instance Scaling Rule briefly names how extra assignable units are gained. Use `slot_level_plus_one_per_level` for Magic Missile-style slot scaling, or `not_applicable` when there is no scaling rule.

fields.label: Target Instance Assignment

log.why: The runtime needs to know whether repeated spell units can stack on one target or must be split.

fields.comment: Target Instance Assignment records whether assignable units can hit the same target, different targets, or independent positions. Use `same_or_different_targets` when the caster can stack all units on one target, split them, or mix them. Use `independent_positions` when instances are placed or moved as separate points rather than assigned to creatures.

fields.label: Target Instance Resolution

log.why: Some repeated spell units resolve at the same time, which affects downstream timing and damage grouping.

fields.comment: Target Instance Resolution records whether the assigned units resolve simultaneously, sequentially, or persist as ongoing spell-created instances. Use `simultaneous` for Magic Missile darts and `persistent` for created lights or similar ongoing instances.

fields.label: Target Instance Notes

log.why: A short note can preserve allocation wording while the typed instance fields stay narrow.

fields.comment: Target Instance Notes preserves a short clarification for assignable target instances without replacing the typed instance count, assignment, and resolution fields.

fields.label: Valid Targets

log.why: Target categories must be machine-readable for spell targeting UIs.
Valid target categories are conditional and still being normalized by the targeting/range bucket.
The live structured targeting rows distinguish concrete target filters beyond the initial broad categories.
Live structured targeting rows include concrete noun filters that are more specific than the initial broad target categories.

fields.comment: Live structured targeting rows include concrete noun filters that are more specific than the initial broad target categories. It maps to `targeting.validTargets[]`. Purpose: Valid Targets exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the canonical markdown prose, including mechanics hidden in descriptive sentences. It maps to runtime JSON path `targeting.validTargets[]`. The value type is `csv-enum`. This is optional or conditional in the runtime shape. Omit or leave empty only when this runtime/template layer intentionally treats the field as optional.
